description_long: This house features a timber-framed exterior with white plaster infill, giving it a classic European or Tudor appearance. The roof is steeply pitched and covered with wooden shingles, and a prominent stone chimney rises from one side. The facade includes a mix of rectangular and arched windows, some with wooden shutters, and a small gabled dormer above the main window. The ground floor is elevated on a stone foundation, with a short set of steps leading to a wooden front door. Decorative wooden beams and trim accentuate the structure, and a wrought iron fence runs along the base of the front wall.
description: A timber-framed, two-story house with a steep shingle roof, stone chimney, and decorative wooden trim. Features include arched and rectangular windows, a gabled dormer, and a stone foundation.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entry door, a gabled dormer window, and a wrought iron fence along the stone foundation.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the steep roof pitch, a single shuttered window, and visible timber framing details.
description_view_2: Rear view with symmetrical window placement, extensive timber framing, and no visible entryway.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing a large window arrangement, stone steps, and a visible wheel detail near the foundation.
attribute_window_arrangement: asymmetrical placement with a mix of rectangular and arched windows
attribute_entryway_design: Wooden door with small steps, decorative timber trim, and a wrought iron fence at the base
